settings.gge9fAeKv0g_text: What is sensitive skin: A clear definition
settings.ggJXnLEnRqH_text: What to look for in a face moisturiser for sensitive skin
settings.ggx-ifyHSbR_text: Ingredients to avoid in a sensitive skin moisturiser
settings.ggGcF2ClLc__text: Best moisturisers for a sensitive face
settings.ggsuc8uMOvV_text:
Sensitive skin can be one of the most difficult skin types to care for. Irritation can flare up quickly, dryness and redness are common, and even seemingly simple products can cause a reaction.

 

Finding the best moisturiser for dry, sensitive skin can really help. The right formula not only helps calm irritation and reduce redness, but also supports your skin’s natural barrier so dryness doesn’t keep coming back.

 

In this guide, we cover the formulas to look out for, the ingredients to avoid, and some of our top face moisturisers for sensitive skin. With the right support, your skin will be thriving in no time!
settings.ggbxIGQz7l3_text: What is sensitive skin: A clear definition
settings.ggw51K8muVj_text:
Sensitive skin isn’t something a doctor or dermatologist diagnoses. It’s simply a label for skin that reacts more strongly to everyday triggers. If you experience tightness, redness, burning, or dryness after using a new product or being in a different type of weather, you most likely have sensitive skin. Other signs include stinging after cleansing, random heat, and rough patches and bumps. These reactions can happen immediately or a few hours after a trigger.

 

In most cases, sensitive skin comes from having a weakened skin barrier. When your barrier isn’t working properly, it struggles to hold onto moisture and defend against irritating ingredients or harsh weather.
settings.ggekPDDJ2c8_text: Common causes of a damaged skin barrier
settings.gg1kZQXZesm_text:
You can damage your skin barrier by over-exfoliating, using harsh cleansers, or using too many active ingredients like retinol and strong chemical exfoliants (AHAs/BHAs) too often. Cold weather and wind can worsen the issue. 

 

Once your barrier is disrupted, your skin becomes more vulnerable; this means products and conditions that never bothered you before can suddenly trigger irritation. This is why finding the right moisturiser for sensitive skin is so important… It helps repair and strengthen your barrier, lock in hydration, and reduce the likelihood of future flare-ups.
settings.ggETGZ8Sv5y_text: What to look for in a moisturiser for sensitive skin
settings.ggQU5aCn5I9_text:
Look out for moisturisers that include ingredients that treat the effects of sensitive skin and the root cause. This includes:

 

  • Niacinamide and peptides which directly strengthen the skin barrier
  • Panthenol which soothes irritation
  • Centella asiatica which reduces redness
  • Squalane which mimics the skin's natural oils
  • Hyaluronic acid which deeply hydrates

 

Korean skincare products are often great choices because they focus on gentle, light, barrier-supporting formulations.
settings.ggh7fP9vFam_text: Ingredients to avoid in a sensitive skin moisturiser
settings.ggeEQJIOEvb_text:
If you suspect you have sensitive skin, you should avoid these ingredients when selecting a moisturiser:

 

  • Alcohols (denatured or SD alcohol). These can dry out the skin and disrupt the barrier.
  • Strong preservatives. Ingredients like formaldehyde releasers can provoke reactions in sensitive skin.
  • Heavy occlusives like petroleum and mineral oil. These can trap heat and worsen redness for some sensitive skin types.
settings.ggqar9569_L_text: Best moisturisers for a sensitive face
settings.ggGZrJGgPQ__text: Here are some of our favourite moisturisers for sensitive skin.
settings.ggEb3LFnl-K_text: 1. Purito Seoul Mighty Bamboo Panthenol Cream
settings.gggil_seT7p_text: Purito Seoul Mighty Bamboo Panthenol Cream is a barrier‑friendly product, packed with panthenol, squalane, and centella to calm irritation and reinforce the skin’s protective layer. It has a lightweight gel‑cream texture that absorbs smoothly without feeling heavy, making it ideal for daily hydration without irritation.
settings.gg9Hv_BToCU_text: 2. Beauty of Joseon Red Bean Water Gel
settings.ggeAh5XSztk_text: Beauty of Joseon Red Bean Water Gel is a refreshing, lightweight, water-based formula that’s ideal for sensitive or dry skin. Infused with red bean extract and soothing peptides, it helps balance moisture levels, calm irritation, and leave skin feeling soft and smooth. Its jelly-like texture absorbs quickly without feeling greasy.
settings.ggRdar2RkDe_text: ETUDE SoonJung 2x Barrier Intensive Cream is a favourite among people struggling with sensitive skin. It focuses on deep barrier repair with panthenol to soothe irritation, madecassoside (a derivative of centella asiatica) to reduce redness, and squalane which locks in moisture.
settings.ggfeigb7Pgf_text: 4. INNISFREE Cherry Blossom Glow Jelly Cream
settings.ggO0mGMQrjT_text: The INNISFREE Cherry Blossom Glow Jelly Cream delivers lightweight, refreshing hydration that’s perfect for sensitive skin. It's easy to rub in and leaves skin soft and smooth. Enriched with niacinamide and cherry blossom leaf extract, it helps calm irritation, brighten, and support the skin barrier for a wonderful glow.
settings.ggycz9dDBu8_text: Pyunkang Yul Balancing Gel is a beautifully simple moisturiser with gentle humectants and soothing botanicals to hydrate and balance the skin without irritation. Its minimalist formula avoids unnecessary fragrances and harsh additives, making it ideal for sensitive or reactive skin.
settings.ggusix--Lyp_text:
Looking after sensitive skin is all about consistency. By using the right moisturiser morning and night, you can keep your skin hydrated, calm irritation, and strengthen your skin barrier over time.

 

Start by applying a pea-sized amount, adjusting slightly for drier areas or flare-ups. Apply on slightly damp skin to lock in moisture, and layer lighter serums first (if you have them), finishing with your cream or gel moisturiser.

 

Build your routine slowly, introducing one new product at a time and patch-testing before committing fully. Don’t forget your neck and chest, which are often sensitive too. Over time, this careful approach keeps your skin calm, comfortable, and resilient.
settings.ggiovkgPj-g_text: What type of cream is good for sensitive skin?
settings.ggIqWBTWyCh_text: The best cream for sensitive skin is one that hydrates, soothes, and supports your skin barrier without causing irritation. Look for lightweight creams, gel-creams, or lotion-like textures that absorb quickly and don’t feel heavy or greasy. Key ingredients to check for include panthenol, centella asiatica, squalane, and hyaluronic acid.
settings.ggmlPtbFRRS_text: Which Korean brand is best for sensitive skin?
settings.ggqkr4J3Gfj_text:
There are many Korean skincare brands that are known for creating gentle, barrier-friendly formulas that work well for sensitive skin: Some of the best include:

  • Purito Seoul, which focuses on soothing, hydrating ingredients that strengthen the skin barrier.
  • Beauty of Joseon, which offers lightweight, calming formulas that reduce redness and irritation.
  • ETUDE, which is known for its gentle creams.
  • INNISFREE, which combines natural extracts with barrier-supporting ingredients.
  • Pyunkang Yul, which offers minimalist formulations that prioritize hydration and skin barrier health.
